requirement: Buyer can initiate the supplier onboarding process by providing basic supplier information.
Internal approval workflow is triggered after supplier onboarding initiation.
Supplier receives an invitation to register with AECI and complete due diligence steps after internal approval.
Supplier information is accurate and current.
Visibility to certifications and contact information is provided.
Multiple channels of managing information are eliminated.
Supplier status can be managed, including purchase block, payment block, deactivation, and reactivation.
Approval workflows involving Procurement, Finance, General Accounting, and Master Data teams are implemented.
Integration touchpoints between Coupa SIM and SAP for supplier creation, updates, and activation are established.
Internal users can register new suppliers in Coupa for urgent or business-critical situations.
Internal users can update existing supplier information in Coupa.
Internal users can change the status of a supplier in Coupa (block purchase, block payment, deactivate, reactivate).
Internal users can extend the supplier onboarding process in Coupa.
Internal users can create a one-time vendor in Coupa with a simplified approval flow and reduced data requirements.
Coupa displays the status of each supplier, including Coupa Supplier Portal (CSP) status.
Coupa supports auto-translation of standard information on the user interface for internal users and suppliers.
